title: Respect strict aliasing and pointer provenance
description: Avoid reinterpreting memory through incompatible pointer types; use memcpy or unions so the optimizer's aliasing assumptions stay valid.
body: Use when reinterpreting bytes, casting between pointer types, or hand-optimizing a loop. Do not type-pun by casting a float* to int* and dereferencing, and do not lie with the restrict qualifier on pointers that actually alias. Actions: copy bytes with memcpy (the compiler folds it) or use a union for punning, mark genuinely non-aliasing pointers restrict to enable vectorization, and compile with -Wstrict-aliasing -fno-strict-aliasing only as a temporary diagnostic. Validation signals include identical results at -O0 and -O2 and a clean -fsanitize=undefined run. Risk boundary: a strict-aliasing violation is UB that only misbehaves at high optimization; if results differ between -O0 and -O2, treat it as a real defect and escalate rather than pinning -O0.
